name: AC-411 — Marsh
description: A ladder of firm causeways over open marsh. Moving on firm ground is twice as fast — so the rails and rungs are the map, and the tussock islands are the only honest shortcuts. Three landmasses hang off the edges: two peninsulas and a lake shore.
ambientRegister: Flat grey, ground-level mist, muffled sound. Water and mud underfoot.
designRationale: Movement-cost map: routes are priced, not walled. The causeway ladder gives clean fast routes that the enemy can read; the tussock chains give slow concealed ones. No one-tiling — every rail and rung is 3–4 wide. The finale's three fronts each own a map edge, and the hub rung is where they collide.

grid.implementation: Fill defaultTile. Apply regions in order (later wins). Apply tileOverrides. Regions flagged interference reduce vision by 2 inside the zone.

tileOverrides.label: Pad cache — Reaper seat, t2 from the N shore

vrath_m2.objectiveLabel: The Syndicate are entrenching the east peninsula. Three prices for the same door: the east rail (fast, watched), the hub rung loop (flanks the neck), or the tussock wade (slow, concealed, cold). Pay one before the works finish.

conditionalElements.element: Aedhrik-violation scene fires at ~60% enemy strength.

player.designNote: Shatter eats the entrenchment works for free — the mission counter
Tussock wade — Charge out of the last island
Pushes defenders off the peninsula neck
Holds the hub rung behind the push

enemy.designNote: The entrencher — kill it and the clock stops
Rock spur overwatch over open marsh
On the peninsula cache — the second clock. Kill the Contractor; starve the rest
Forward post on the east rail
Tussock picket — your concealed route isn't free

economyNotes.player: No Reaper — deliberate. The mission punishes economy play; spend what you brought before the works finish.
economyNotes.enemy: Assessor on the peninsula cache: their losses regrow while the walls rise. The clock is double — entrenchment AND rebuy.

vrath_m3.objectiveLabel: Covenant land on the west shoreline; Syndicate press from the south peninsula. You hold the hub. Break one front before the other reaches the rails — then survive turn 4.

conditionalElements.element: Turn 3: the challenge-runner. Turn 4: Vorhel's defection opens a third front inside your own line (east rail).
Pirate-unlock alt: pirates reinforce if every Vrakthi defects.

player.designNote: Throws the landing back into the lake
Hub fortifications — Reinforced builds hold the rung
Rail response force — redeploys to whichever front cracks
Pad cache — seats by t2, keeps the war fund alive

reinforcements.designNote: Vorhel's defectors — your own shapes, wrong color

economyNotes.player: Pad cache is inside the deploy zone — Reaper seats by t2. 50/turn, rebuy at 150–175, respawn at the pad-side Reaper.
economyNotes.enemy: Two fixed fronts plus the defection — no enemy economy. Survive the spend and the marsh is yours.
